name: Indrik
description: The Indrik (also known as Indrik-Beast) is a wondrous beast in Russian medieval folklore, described as a gigantic bull with a horse's head, enormous legs, and one horn protruding from its forehead. It is considered the king of all beasts and dwells in the Holy Mountain. The Indrik possesses immense strength and wisdom. It walks freely where no other creature can tread, digging tunnels with its horn, and its movements are said to cause earthquakes. Unlike many mythical beasts, the Indrik is not inherently malevolent. It is often depicted as a benevolent creature, a wise and powerful protector of the animal kingdom. Some interpretations portray it as a symbolic representation of the earth's power or a guardian of hidden knowledge. The Indrik is believed to possess healing properties, and its horn is a highly sought-after artifact. Stories about the Indrik highlight its peaceful nature, powerful presence, and its role as a protector of the natural world, embodying the strength and mystery of the Slavic wilderness.
subtitle: King of Beasts, Horned Giant
appearance: The Indrik is typically described as a massive bull-like creature. It has the head of a horse, a single large horn projecting from its forehead, and enormous legs capable of traversing any terrain. Its hide is often depicted as being stone-like or covered in moss, blending in with its natural surroundings. Some accounts describe it as having iridescent fur or scales.
habitat: The Holy Mountain, underground tunnels, remote forests
behavior: The Indrik is generally peaceful and solitary. It spends its time wandering through its domain, protecting other animals and maintaining the balance of nature. It is wise and observant, offering guidance to those who seek it. When provoked, it can unleash its immense power, causing earthquakes and landslides.

keyMyths.title: The Indrik and the Holy Mountain
keyMyths.description: The Indrik resides on the Holy Mountain, a place of immense power and spiritual significance. It is said to guard the mountain's secrets and protect it from those who would exploit its resources. The mountain is its source of power.

sources.author: W.R.S. Ralston
sources.description: A collection of Russian folktales, including stories about the Indrik.
sources.text: A collection of Russian folktales, including stories about the Indrik.
